title: acinos alpinus subsp. nomismophyllus
intro: Acinos alpinus subsp. nomismophyllus, also known as alpine savory or mountain savory, is a subspecies of the Acinos alpinus plant, a low-growing, perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including terpenes and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Acinos alpinus subsp. nomismophyllus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Acinos alpinus subsp. nomismophyllus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
